[2020-05-03 00:34] flakmaniak: So for base+N, which Ns are considered best/most standard?
[2020-05-03 00:34] flakmaniak: Base + 5 feels definitely too few units.
[2020-05-03 00:34] flakmaniak: I used to think base + 8 was normal and 11 seemed overwhelming...
[2020-05-03 00:35] flakmaniak: But now, after playing lots of ranked where bonus unit cards just inflict more on me... I dunno. 8, 9, 10, whatever. Is there such a thing as too many extra units?
[2020-05-03 00:35] flakmaniak: I don't think I'd want to play fewer than base + 8.

[2020-05-03 11:43] Deleted User (Deleted User): in fact pris is rather simple
[2020-05-03 11:43] flakmaniak: It's not that it's hard and complex. In fact, I think Prismata's rules are pretty simple and easy to teach compared to a lot of e.g. card games that have very complex rules and card sets.
[2020-05-03 11:44] flakmaniak: Nor is it really that the strategy is hard; that part is true, but I don't think it's what makes the game niche.
[2020-05-03 11:45] flakmaniak: It's that, being a deterministic strategy game, it lacks a lot of that visceral excitement that you'd get from a real-time game (an FPS, a fighting game, etc.), or from the big, surprising moments in a card game. You have to work to find the fun and interesting parts in Prismata. If you don't get it, you'll just sort of vaguely wonder why you lost.
[2020-05-03 11:46] flakmaniak: If I'm showing Netrunner to someone, they will quickly understand the notion of "Is this advanced card in the remote an agenda, or a horrible trap?", and they'll get that that's the focus of this turn.
[2020-05-03 11:47] flakmaniak: Or things like, maybe they ran recklessly, and faceplanted some huge ICE.
[2020-05-03 11:47] flakmaniak: And it's clear why that happened, and what risk they were taking when they ran.
[2020-05-03 11:47] flakmaniak: It's clear why it's exciting to run on facedown ICE.
[2020-05-03 11:49] flakmaniak: I guess what I'm saying is, in a card game, it can often come down to one big moment, where you made a specific decision that changed the outcome, right before the game ended. As opposed to Prismata where you feel more like the die was cast a bunch of turns ago, and you see who gets breached first, who was more right about the set.
[2020-05-03 11:49] flakmaniak: (Ok maybe most card games aren't nearly as good as Netrunner about having this kind of agency as the game goes on.)
[2020-05-03 11:52] flakmaniak: Hmm. I guess I should amend and say that the strategy in Prismata being somewhat opaque CAN be an issue. But I don't think it's the primary issue.
[2020-05-03 11:52] flakmaniak: And, again, Prismata's core rules are, I think, very streamlined.
[2020-05-03 11:53] flakmaniak: And it's not hard for people to understand how they work.
[2020-05-03 11:55] flakmaniak: This isn't a game where I expect people to have a bunch of rules questions, to misunderstand a bunch of interactions. It's not like Netrunner, with its bafflingly strange and unique core gameplay, or like L5R, with five different ring effects you have to remember at all times, an overwrought timing structure, etc.
[2020-05-03 11:55] flakmaniak: (Maybe the big digital card games are way way way simpler than these offline card games, but again, I think Prismata is more than simple enough, ruleswise.)

[2020-05-03 13:18] flakmaniak: I agree completely MasN. That has been one of my big complaints about Netrunner in the past few years.
[2020-05-03 13:18] flakmaniak: Be it Tracker, IP Block, Fairchild3, etc.
[2020-05-03 13:19] flakmaniak: As you say, it used to be that the efficient taxing ICE was safe to bounce off of, so you'd run aggressively to get them to spend money.
[2020-05-03 13:19] flakmaniak: Or the dangerous ICE wasn't taxing.
[2020-05-03 13:19] flakmaniak: But then you get dumb cards like IP Block that are just very efficient and nigh-guaranteed +tempo in all circumstances.
[2020-05-03 13:19] flakmaniak: Instead of being -tempo like taxing ICE should.
[2020-05-03 13:20] flakmaniak: It's made even worse by DNA Tracker being a Code Gate (and Fairchild3 to a lesser extent). Used to be that it was "get your Killer and be reasonably safe", but since Tracker that's just not the case.
[2020-05-03 13:21] flakmaniak: Also, with Tracker it's the fact that not only is it really taxing AND punishing on facecheck... It punishes the runner's tempo in a similar way to if they'd broken it, if they just run through it.
[2020-05-03 13:21] flakmaniak: Because it removes so much money.
[2020-05-03 13:21] flakmaniak: That there's so little counterplay other than "don't run".
[2020-05-03 13:22] flakmaniak: Also, even Surveyor can do this, making you pay a lot to bounce off of it. I mean Surveyor is a bit much for various reasons. But that's one of them.
[2020-05-03 13:23] flakmaniak: I've joked before that by deleting one ICE per faction I could make the game significantly nicer on this dimension. Just delete Tracker, Fairchild3, Surveyor, and IP Block. (Ok the joke is that NBN has a nigh-infinite stable of pretty good ICE in this direction.)
[2020-05-03 13:24] flakmaniak: Even cards like Slot Machine are frustrating, because it's just... It's just -tempo to run it. And there's basically no way to do it efficiently.

[2020-05-03 13:36] flakmaniak: Back to Prismata a moment, is there any info on the asymmetry rate in base + N as N changes? As in, are you more likely to have significantly asymmetrical games in base + 8 than in base + 5? Or base + 11?
[2020-05-03 13:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): mhm, i was voicing a completely legitimate concern
[2020-05-03 13:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): don't let the last line throw you off
[2020-05-03 13:37] shadourow (Rando): Eh, you think that I read anything but the CAPS LOCKED line ?
[2020-05-03 13:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): i also hate NGO Front for a similar reason:
normally if you want to bait the runner into running a taxing server, you either have to IAA a real agenda and risk it getting stolen, or devote card slots to an ambush in your deck and the entire IAA gets wasted if the runner chooses to not hit it
[2020-05-03 13:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): now you have NGO front which is hedge fund level efficiency
[2020-05-03 13:39] flakmaniak: NGO Front is pretty boring, yeah. It's "too easy" for the corp to include and use.
[2020-05-03 13:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): they don't run, you get your hedge fund, they do run you made them pay though their ass to get to it and you still get your hedge fund
[2020-05-03 13:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): there's like no risk to it
[2020-05-03 13:39] flakmaniak: Whereas with an AggSec or an Overwriter as the corp you are sweating bullets the whole time.
[2020-05-03 13:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): also the jinteki id that gives you a free install on the runner's turn is stupid
[2020-05-03 13:40] flakmaniak: NGO Front is the actuality of the risk that cards like GRNDL Refinery posed, where if they were too good, it would be a "damned if you do, damned if you don't" for the runner. I mean you're not damned if you don't run NGO. But still.
[2020-05-03 13:41] flakmaniak: That ID got banned, thankfully.
[2020-05-03 13:41] flakmaniak: No excuse for having printed it; it was obviously WAY over the line.
[2020-05-03 13:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): if the ID is so good that the runner is digging really hard for an employee strike, why the heck is it also 45/15
[2020-05-03 13:41] flakmaniak: You play like half a game with it and you're already like "this is stupid".
[2020-05-03 13:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): i feel dirty every time i IA an NGO front with it
[2020-05-03 13:41] flakmaniak: It wasn't subtle at all how powerful it was.
[2020-05-03 13:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): they run it every time because they think its a nisei
[2020-05-03 13:41] flakmaniak: The corp has literally installed zero ICE on turn 1 and you're like "I can't run".
[2020-05-03 13:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): and then i hit them with the DNA tracker, and they aren't even allowed to jack out
[2020-05-03 13:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): and some people even run brainstorm
[2020-05-03 13:42] flakmaniak: They'll have an agenda naked on the table and you'll be like "Well, guess I can't do shit here".
[2020-05-03 13:42] flakmaniak: It's like, sure, Tracker, Anansi, blah blah. Or just... Cortex Lock?
[2020-05-03 13:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): thankfully, it was never legal with capcrice nisei in the same deck
[2020-05-03 13:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): that interaction is fucking stupid
[2020-05-03 13:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): nisei triggers twice
[2020-05-03 13:42] flakmaniak: I always ran it with Grail; being able to put whatever ICE type they don't have a breaker for, and then trash all their programs...
[2020-05-03 13:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): ive run it with grail, ive run it with conventional ice, its just good
[2020-05-03 13:43] flakmaniak: Anyway yeah, I'm glad Mti Mwekundu got banned to hell and back; it just should have been banned a year prior, during development.
[2020-05-03 13:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): it didnt get banned for way too long
[2020-05-03 13:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): i played against it, im like "oh wow that seems pretty good", i played games with it, im like "oh wow this is stupid", i played more games against it, i quit
[2020-05-03 13:44] flakmaniak: Yeah there were a couple months or something of "Wait, is this actually almost fair?", and uh, spoilers, it wasn't, everyone's day 1 impression, from literally their first game against it, was correct.
[2020-05-03 13:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): maybe it got banned after?
[2020-05-03 13:44] flakmaniak: It turns out that the game is more fair when the corp has to spend clicks and cards and credits to defend their servers!
[2020-05-03 13:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): i think after sansan/data and destiny was the best time for netrunner
[2020-05-03 13:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): politicals are stupid
[2020-05-03 13:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): prison decks are the most NPEs ever
[2020-05-03 13:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): you can't even just give up because there's a slim chance you lucksack a win
[2020-05-03 13:44] flakmaniak: Also it's like, even if it JUST gave you the later-game efficiency of saving so many clicks and credits on installs, that's pretty good. But then it ALSO makes the early game giga-oppressive.
[2020-05-03 13:45] flakmaniak: I think most of the political assets got banned.
[2020-05-03 13:45] masn6811 (Masne98888): they were legal for way too long
[2020-05-03 13:45] flakmaniak: Thankfully I haven't seen the net damage one in ages.
[2020-05-03 13:45] masn6811 (Masne98888): they should have gotten banned like 1 week after the first successful prison deck showed up
[2020-05-03 13:45] masn6811 (Masne98888): spoiler: they did not
[2020-05-03 13:46] flakmaniak: There was about five minutes some months ago when an RP deck with the net damage political was the deck du jour because it was featured on nrdb, but everyone gave up on it after a week or two because we didn't see the same magic in it that its creator did.
[2020-05-03 13:47] flakmaniak: I still love pre-Order and Chaos Netrunner. Or I guess just after Order and Chaos is fine. Don't even need the SanSan cycle cards. That's the era I'm most nostalgic for.
[2020-05-03 13:47] flakmaniak: But probably it's better to have Clot in the game.
[2020-05-03 13:47] masn6811 (Masne98888): if i do have something positive to say about mti, is it made mumbad city grid actually usable, and quite fun to use. as a corp, that is
[2020-05-03 13:47] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you keep sliding a grail, it gains 2 more subs each time

[2020-05-03 13:47] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you keep sliding komainu, it also keeps gaining subs
[2020-05-03 13:47] masn6811 (Masne98888): or you can just slam them with an 8 str surveyor a bunch
[2020-05-03 13:47] flakmaniak: I think Mumbad City Grid was almost playable even before that. I played it in Grail Palana a little, right after first rotation.
[2020-05-03 13:48] masn6811 (Masne98888): i still think the idea of it is kinda stupid
[2020-05-03 13:48] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean palana and dna tracker
[2020-05-03 13:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): like you play like a normal glacier with a econ id and good taxing ice
[2020-05-03 13:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you want to play a normal glacier, HB already exists
[2020-05-03 13:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): i like how each faction plays differently
[2020-05-03 13:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): RP glacier is very distinct from HB glacier
[2020-05-03 13:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): palana glacier is mostly the same thing
[2020-05-03 13:50] flakmaniak: It's true that Palana was a bit EtF. That said, at least in the old days, EtF glacier might make more remotes for Adonis/Eve, whereas Palana tended to be exactly one ever.
[2020-05-03 13:50] flakmaniak: Man I miss making several remotes in non-asset-spam decks.
[2020-05-03 13:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean, HB glacier runs campaigns, usually with breaker bay, palana doesn't, and the way you gain credits is keyed on drawing, which goes with the net damage theme, but still its very similar
[2020-05-03 13:50] flakmaniak: I think that asset spam got out of control, and then the anti-asset cards got out of control as well, and therefore playing "a normal level of assets" couldn't really be a thing anymore.
[2020-05-03 13:51] flakmaniak: Remember when random corp decks could just play PAD Campaign?
[2020-05-03 13:51] flakmaniak: It feels like a lifetime ago.
[2020-05-03 13:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): but honestly speaking
[2020-05-03 13:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): i think PAD campaign was really just bad
[2020-05-03 13:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): its worth trashing for the runner if uniced
[2020-05-03 13:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): and its such a small effect you dont want to devote an ice to it
[2020-05-03 13:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): nobody realized it then
[2020-05-03 13:52] masn6811 (Masne98888): it took thebigboy some time to put the pieces together to make dumblefork
[2020-05-03 13:52] flakmaniak: Yeah, it is interesting in retrospect that Whizzard took so long to catch on.
[2020-05-03 13:52] masn6811 (Masne98888): but anyone else could have made it before then
[2020-05-03 13:52] flakmaniak: Even when NEH fast advance with a lot of un-ICEd assets was the deck to beat.
[2020-05-03 13:53] flakmaniak: On the one hand, those ancient NEH decks were definitely a little too powerful, a bit too fast. But on the other... I dunno, one still had pretty interesting games against them. I don't mind going back in time and facing that sort of deck.
[2020-05-03 13:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): i wish corps could have a hand size more than 5
[2020-05-03 13:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): like every card that increased hand size was never playable
[2020-05-03 13:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): research station: unplayable
[2020-05-03 13:54] flakmaniak: I think Cybernetics Court sees some fringe play.
[2020-05-03 13:54] flakmaniak: (Also... Cerebral Imaging?)
[2020-05-03 13:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): but i tried it in a super glaicer blue sun. it was okay in that very specific deck
[2020-05-03 13:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): TWIY* only used as a 40 card id for FA before NEH existed
[2020-05-03 13:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): CI: that's the main exception
[2020-05-03 13:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): cybernetics court: fringe play
[2020-05-03 13:54] flakmaniak: I somewhat enjoyed the fact that CI told you "play something degenerate", but not what. It's like, the opponent sits down with CI, and you know they're up to SOME horrible bullshit, but you have no idea what.
[2020-05-03 13:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): and they recently released an NBN agenda that increases hand size. trash
[2020-05-03 13:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): I think CI is fairly cool and unique
[2020-05-03 13:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): it's just that VLC has a huge drawback of making you discard
[2020-05-03 13:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): and CI makes it too good
[2020-05-03 13:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): CI was quite fine before VLC existed
[2020-05-03 13:56] flakmaniak: I don't think I've seen a combo card in other games before that says "you must play some horrible combo deck, but you have five billion options as to what". Usually the weird combo cards push you in a very specific direction.
[2020-05-03 13:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): i also hate the power shutdown erreta. power shutdown combos were fun
[2020-05-03 13:56] flakmaniak: I mean I guess there were SOME non-combo CI decks.
[2020-05-03 13:56] flakmaniak: Was Power Shutdown combo actually getting out of hand when they issued that errata?
[2020-05-03 13:56] flakmaniak: I always thought it was a pretty good power level back in the day.
[2020-05-03 13:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): i tried CI as a glacier with ashigaru: spoiler: not good
[2020-05-03 13:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): it was not out of hand at all
[2020-05-03 13:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): it was very balanced in blue sun
[2020-05-03 13:56] flakmaniak: Kind of miraculous, really, that that Power Shutdown combo was good enough to play sometimes, but really quite fair overall.

[2020-05-03 13:58] flakmaniak: It's like, no Fracter ever needs to be better than Corroder. Corroder was perfectly fair; no corp will ever tell you it's too strong, and no runner should ever tell you it's too weak.

[2020-05-03 18:53] awaclus: compare the results of killing them and letting opponent block with their blockers
[2020-05-03 18:53] awaclus: e.g. if you have 3 attack and opponent has a Wild, an Engi and a Wall, consider whether it's better to get rid of a Wild or get rid of an Engi
[2020-05-03 18:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): spoiler alert: it's usually the wild
[2020-05-03 18:56] awaclus: if you're not sure, I feel like a decent rule of thumb is that denying absorb is good, but if you're not denying absorb, it's better to attack blockers than wilds
[2020-05-03 18:57] awaclus: with a very large number of wilds, it can be better to attack blockers even if you are denying like 1 or 2 absorb by attacking wilds
[2020-05-03 18:57] zera777 (Zera): Killing a Wild is like killing a Forcefield, except Wilds can't protect the actual Drones.
[2020-05-03 18:58] awaclus: killing a Wild is like killing an Ossi
[2020-05-03 18:58] awaclus: Forcefields don't produce gold
[2020-05-03 18:59] zera777 (Zera): Force your opponent to build Forcefields, then kill their Wild Drones instead, inflicting maximum economic damage.
